author: assistant
conditions: Any new audit emission site must classify its findings explicitly using the shared vocabulary; unclassified findings cannot influence outcomes.
Reopening or superseding this gap requires new evidence of vocabulary divergence or a new consumer bypassing the shared module.
reason: VERIFY-20260719-0003 records commit-level evidence for both milestones, the acyclic dependency direction (both consumers import the vocabulary; the vocabulary imports neither), identical vocabulary, severity policy, and rollup across all three consumers, fixture coverage of the original zip-export incident scenario now reporting unverifiable with zero errors, 580 passing tests, a verified_current repository audit, and independent cross-environment reproduction. COMP-0031 records unanimous provider recommendation to close GAP-0004 citing exactly this evidence chain.
scope: Closure warrant for GAP-0004. Warrants closing the gap on the evidence of VERIFY-20260719-0003: both milestones are complete and the deficiency is resolved. The self-model index and registry audits distinguish confirmed drift from unavailable evidence via the shared three-valued vocabulary (milestone 1: PLAN-20260719-0001, CAP-0013), and the sidecar contract adopts the same vocabulary for its staleness assessment (milestone 2: PLAN-20260717-0001, CAP-0014), eliminating the risk of two incompatible verification vocabularies. Registry-audit unverifiable outcomes remain unreachable by documented scope (the registry audit performs no git-evidence verification), adjudicated as justified inapplicability in WARR-20260719-0002. Future evidence-dependent registry checks or new audit emission sites requiring vocabulary changes need new governance, not this gap.
